title: Introduction to the EXAFS technique: exercises
author: dacapito, francesco
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This hands-on lecture guides users through the  practical steps of analyzing EXAFS (Extended X-ray Absorption Fine Structure) data . It covers: 
 
  Software & Data Retrieval:  Use of the  Larch suite  (open-source) for data processing, and accessing experimental data from the  LISA XAS Database . 
  Data Pre-treatment:  Alignment, averaging, deglitching, and background subtraction to extract the EXAFS signal. 
  Signal Extraction & Normalization:  Determining the edge position, jump height, and isolating the oscillatory part of the spectrum. 
  Modeling & Fitting:  Building theoretical models using crystallographic data (e.g., from the  Crystallography Open Database ), followed by  1st shell and multi-shell fitting  to refine structural parameters (N, R, ΔE₀, σ²). 
  Visualization & Analysis:  Using tools for data visualization, linear combination fitting (LCF), and XANES peak fitting. 
 
 The lecture emphasizes iterative refinement and careful selection of scattering paths for accurate structural analysis. Ideal for researchers and students working with synchrotron-based XAS data.
